Slot: EAR
AC: 10
STR: +9
SV MAGIC: +10
Effect: See Invisible (Worn, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 0.0 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Lore Item
Slot: EAR
AC: 10
STR: +9
SV MAGIC: +10
Effect: See Invisible (Worn, Casting Time: Instant)
WT: 0.0 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Average Price: 20,000pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Very Rare
Level to Attain: 55

QuoteReply
# Nov 16 2002 at 7:30 AM Rating: Default

377 posts
Our guild killed Vindi for the first time yesterday and the Living Thunder Earring dropped. We were quite disappointed cause we were hoping for the breastplate or boots, heh.
▲ Page top
RE: Lucky?!
QuoteReply
# Apr 25 2003 at 10:44 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591788653851
52 posts
If you have the force to kill Vindi then be happy, many breastplates and boots are in your future, but be happier that you saw the earring. The only time I have -ever- seen it drop, it was awarded to me, and I'll never let it go due to great sentamentle value. It's great if you dont have a clicky see-invis, great for pulling if your a warrior, and probably useful to monks and SK's as well as bards and any other classes that pull. I'm not sure because I have never played any of those for any realy amount of time.
▲ Page top
Prospective
QuoteReply
# Sep 29 2002 at 12:12 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592251198679
51 posts
Just keep things in prospective here:
Remember, we discovered this thing right after Velious came out...at the time, this item was so incredibly ubber that everyone stared at awe in this incredible item. Its MR and See Invis only compounded its value on my server- Rallos Zek- making it...just...well, there was nothing to begin to compare with it. Now we have the Druidic cabage quest with the FT earring (EoS) for all races, earrings and items of this type seem much more within reason. I forget its name- there's a robe now that's Flowing Thought 10, +100 all saves, +30 int, +5wis/some other stat, +some mana (and HP?), like 60 or 80AC, incredible items are just taken for granted...
this thing was the Holy Grail! Haha, just an EQ ancient going on about forgotten times when noobs really really wanted fine steel weapons and last names were great... haha, peace all. Remember that this thing came out in an era that astounded us all!

Rarity
QuoteReply
# Mar 25 2002 at 10:31 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592246950953
3 posts
We killed Derakor the Vindicator over 30x, and I've seen this Earring drop ONCE. It is insanely rare, and the Vindicator requires a decent size force to kill. Expect to see a TON of boots and breastplates before you see even one of these, and then good luck on getting to loot/keep it.
▲ Page top
Level 55 Monk's Opinion
QuoteReply
# Jan 16 2002 at 4:22 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591757889512
Sage
72 posts
Is this a "monk item"?

The answer to that is "yes and no".

If you define a monk item as something that should default automatically to a monk before any other classes that want it, then it is NOT a monk item. Earrings are almost always light-weight, so the light weight of this item means nothing as far as making it a monk item. This earring would be considered a very nice earring by almost anybody that can use it. It would almost certainly end up on melee classes before casters however. If perchance 95% of all earrings weighed five pounds each, then this might qualify as a "monk item", but they don't and it doesn't.

If you define a monk item as something that would be great for a monk to have, then yes, this is a "monk item". My guild has killed the Vindicator once. We plan to kill him more in the future. Maybe I will get one of these earrings one day, but it won't be just because I am a monk.

Is there such a thing as a "monk item", i.e. an item that should automatically go to a monk? Yes, there is. There is also such a thing as an "enchanter item", a "cleric item", a "warrior item", a "rogue item", etc. Other classes just don't get as excited and vocal about discovering an item that is good for them as monks do because of our severe equipment restrictions.

One thing that many people can't seem to understand is that "ALL/ALL" on an item don't mean jack at the high-end of the game. If this earring had 3 AC, +20 Charisma, and +100 mana, even if it said "ALL/ALL", a monk would NEVER get it. It would go to an enchanter because it would be an "enchanter item". I have even seen people suggest random rolling on items such as this. The only time random rolling would be done on an item like this would be if the people in charge of loot couldn't decide between two or three finalists for possession of the earring.

Can monks be annoying with all these "monk item" comments? Yes, sometimes they annoy even me. Sometimes I even agree with them and they still annoy me.

Do people over-react to "monk item" comments and display the maturity of your average eight year old child? Do they heap unwarranted abuse on anyone who dares to use that hated phrase? Can they be even more annoying than the person who posted the "monk item" comment in the first place? The answers to these questions are: "Yes", "Yes", and "Emphatically Yes!"
▲ Page top
RE: Level 55 Monk's Opinion
QuoteReply
# May 15 2002 at 11:31 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591774257848
20 posts
As a lvl 55 monk myself i would say no. Just from it not haveing hps or sta to add HPs. Str is EAZY to get sv maigc is nice, but as the only save i wouldn't get it for that. The ac is nice, but not that nice. Anyways theres another 55 monk opinion. =)
